clause_anchor.span: Each Major Investor has a pro rata right to purchase its share of every security issued by the Company, including securities issued under equity incentive plans, as acquisition consideration, in strategic transactions, in equipment or lease financing, with debt financing, or in a public offering.

deviations.original_text: Each Major Investor has a pro rata right to purchase its share of new securities issued by the Company, excluding securities issued under equity incentive plans, as acquisition consideration, in strategic transactions, in equipment or lease financing, with debt financing, or in a public offering.
deviations.mutated_text: Each Major Investor has a pro rata right to purchase its share of every security issued by the Company, including securities issued under equity incentive plans, as acquisition consideration, in strategic transactions, in equipment or lease financing, with debt financing, or in a public offering.

clause_anchor.span: Any side-letter MFN right automatically imports any more favorable economic, information, governance, veto, board, transfer, operational consent, or other investor right granted to any other investor after the date of this Agreement.

deviations.original_text: Any side-letter MFN right applies only to the economic fee terms and information delivery dates expressly listed in the applicable side letter. No MFN imports governance, veto, board, transfer, operational consent, or other investor rights unless the Company expressly agrees in a signed amendment.
deviations.mutated_text: Any side-letter MFN right automatically imports any more favorable economic, information, governance, veto, board, transfer, operational consent, or other investor right granted to any other investor after the date of this Agreement.

clause_anchor.span: Before any holder transfers shares other than to a Permitted Transferee, the holder must first offer the shares to the Major Investors on the same terms stated in the transfer notice. Only if the Major Investors decline or fail to purchase all offered shares may the Company exercise a secondary purchase right.

deviations.original_text: Before any holder transfers shares other than to a Permitted Transferee, the holder must first offer the shares to the Company on the same terms stated in the transfer notice. Only if the Company declines or fails to purchase all offered shares may eligible investors exercise any secondary purchase right.
deviations.mutated_text: Before any holder transfers shares other than to a Permitted Transferee, the holder must first offer the shares to the Major Investors on the same terms stated in the transfer notice. Only if the Major Investors decline or fail to purchase all offered shares may the Company exercise a secondary purchase right.

clause_anchor.span: "Major Investor" means a holder of at least 100,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock. "Requisite Holders" means any single Major Investor holding at least 10% of the outstanding Series A Preferred Stock.

deviations.original_text: "Major Investor" means a holder of at least 1,000,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock. "Requisite Holders" means Major Investors holding at least a majority of the outstanding Series A Preferred Stock, voting as a single class.
deviations.mutated_text: "Major Investor" means a holder of at least 100,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock. "Requisite Holders" means any single Major Investor holding at least 10% of the outstanding Series A Preferred Stock.

clause_anchor.span: Except for the closed list in Section 4, management may conduct the Company's ordinary-course operations, including budgets within board-approved limits, hiring, vendor contracts, product decisions, customer arrangements, and routine financing administration. Notwithstanding Section 4, the Company will obtain Requisite Holder approval for any other matter that an investor reasonably identifies as material to its investment.

deviations.original_text: Except for the closed list in Section 4, management may conduct the Company's ordinary-course operations without investor consent, including budgets within board-approved limits, hiring, vendor contracts, product decisions, customer arrangements, and routine financing administration.
deviations.mutated_text: Except for the closed list in Section 4, management may conduct the Company's ordinary-course operations, including budgets within board-approved limits, hiring, vendor contracts, product decisions, customer arrangements, and routine financing administration. Notwithstanding Section 4, the Company will obtain Requisite Holder approval for any other matter that an investor reasonably identifies as material to its investment.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
distractors.why_benign: The governing-law canary says Delaware law is acceptable for this engagement.

distractors.span: Any dispute arising under this Agreement will be resolved by confidential arbitration before AAA, except that either party may seek temporary or preliminary injunctive relief in a court of competent jurisdiction.
distractors.why_benign: The dispute-resolution canary says confidential arbitration is acceptable for this engagement.

distractors.span: The Company has issued Common Stock and Series A Preferred Stock, and each holder agrees to exercise rights under this Agreement in good faith.
distractors.why_benign: Describing the capital structure and good-faith exercise of rights does not conflict with the company-side playbook.
